title: NOC 21231 – Software Engineers and Designers | Canada Immigration with GTR Immigration
description: Software Engineers and Designers are among the most sought-after tech professionals across Canada, especially in innovation hubs such as Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. Classified under NOC 21231 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for Express-Entry-linked programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) pathways.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2123 – Computer, software, and web designers and developers

duties: Design, develop, and test computer-based systems and mobile apps
Build data, process, and network models
Troubleshoot and upgrade operating systems and applications
Support embedded and process-control software systems
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, software engineering, systems engineering, mathematics, or related field
Possible master’s or doctoral degree for advanced roles
Professional Engineer (P. Eng.) licensing when approving engineering designs
Completion of supervised work experience and professional exams for engineering registration
job_outlook: This keeps the occupation immigration-friendly, particularly for overseas professionals with:
additional_information: Canada’s digital-economy expansion, cloud adoption, AI development, and fintech growth continue to drive recruitment for:
Enterprise-software engineersCloud-infrastructure specialistsEmbedded-systems designersMobile-app developersSite-reliability engineers
Typical annual salaries range from $56,834–$138,758 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and seniority.
